what_it_does: Records a channel-memory attachment entry that links attachment att-8d288e65 in the dragnet-development channel to board item item-5b9e1c6a via the board_items attachment type.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It declares an attachment id, channel, attachment_type, target URI, and added timestamp.
The target is a board:// URI: board://item-5b9e1c6a.
The added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone: 2026-06-24T05:12:10.654075+00:00.
standouts: Use of a board:// URI scheme to reference a target board item.
Attachment type is board_items, implying a cross-reference between channel memory and a board entity.
conventions: Channel is referenced by a slug-style identifier.
Timestamp is ISO 8601 with explicit timezone.
concerns: No body or content describing what is attached.
No author, editor, or modification history.
The target board item is referenced only by URI with no title or context.
notes: This file is a minimal ledger entry that ties a channel attachment to a board item in dragnet-development, useful only as a linkage record.
questions: What content or message does this attachment represent?
What is the nature or title of board item item-5b9e1c6a?
How is this attachment consumed or rendered by the system?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a board_items attachment record and it points to the declared board:// target.
apparent_themes: Traceability between channel memory and board items.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a durable, typed, timestamped link between a channel-memory artifact and a board item so the relationship can be queried or surfaced later.
contribution: Provides a typed, timestamped, URI-based reference from a channel attachment to a board item, supporting graph traversal between channel memory and board entities in a fused ledger.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML metadata attachment record (att-8d288e65) in the dragnet-development channel of type board_items targeting board://item-5b9e1c6a, created on 2026-06-24.
intention_for_embedding: To persist a typed link between a channel-memory attachment and a specific board item for later retrieval and cross-reference.
